summary: DHH reports on Jason Fried's successful SXSW presentation about making big things happen with small teams, based on the Building of Basecamp workshop. The talk drew roughly 180 people to an overcrowded room. An attendee praised Jason as the best speaker of the day, highlighting his charisma and expertise. DHH notes that their upcoming Chicago workshop is already sold out, suggesting they could have easily filled another one given the audience enthusiasm.
key_points: Jason Fried's SXSW talk on small teams building big things drew an overflowing crowd of 180 people
The talk distilled an hour of insight from the Building of Basecamp workshop
Attendees praised Jason as the best and most charismatic speaker at SXSW that day
The upcoming Chicago Building of Basecamp workshop was already sold out, demonstrating strong demand for 37signals' approach
money_quotes: How to make big things happen with small teams
Around 180 people were trying to fit in the obviously way to small room
I bet Jason could have sold out another workshop by the engrossed audience
key_insight: The small-team philosophy that 37signals championed with Basecamp was already generating massive interest and demand at major tech conferences in 2005.
